name: Cabo de Cope Trail (PR-MU 104)
name_original: Parque Regional del Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre — Vuelta al Cocón de Cope (PR-MU 104)
region: Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre
description: Hike the moderate 4.7-mile PR-MU 104 trail in Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre, gaining 1000 feet for panoramic views from Cocón de Cope.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre offer a diverse coastal landscape characterized by dramatic cliffs, pristine beaches, and unique geological formations where Mediterranean mountains meet the sea. The region features a fascinating mix of limestone in Cabo Cope and phyllite rocks in Calnegre, along with fossil dunes and hidden coves. This protected coastal area provides varied terrain for outdoor activities, including salt marshes and dunes that contribute to its ecological diversity. Hikers can explore 17 kilometers of coastline, offering panoramic views and historical landmarks. Best family friendly hiking trails in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre The most popular family friendly hiking route is Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23), a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This easy route offers coastal views and is suitable for families. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cope Tower – Cerro de la Cabrica Viewpoint loop from Torre de Cope, an easy 2.5 miles (4.0 km) path. This trail includes a visit to the historic Cope Tower and offers panoramic vistas. Local hikers also love the View of Cabo Cope – View of Isla del Fraile loop from Calabardina, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through coastal scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre is defined by coastal paths, diverse geological formations, and historical watchtowers. The network offers options for different ability levels, with many routes suitable for families. The routes in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are there in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre?
A: The region offers a wonderful selection of family-friendly trails. You'll find 23 routes suitable for families, with 11 of them being easy and 12 of moderate difficulty, ensuring there's something for every age and energy level.

Q: Are there any easy, short walks suitable for families with young children?
A: Yes, for families with younger children or those looking for a gentle stroll, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899539531/tour-along-the-cope-marina-trail-sl-mu-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. It's an easy route, just under 5 kilometers, offering a pleasant experience along the coast.

Q: What kind of natural features can we expect to see on family hikes in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre?
A: Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre boasts a diverse landscape where Mediterranean mountains meet the sea. You'll encounter dramatic cliffs, hidden coves, and pristine beaches. The geological variety, with limestone and phyllite rocks, fossil dunes, and salt marshes, creates a visually interesting terrain. The area is also a Special Protection Area for Birds, so keep an eye out for diverse wildlife.

Q: Are there any circular routes that are good for families?
A: Absolutely! Many of the family-friendly trails are circular, making them convenient for returning to your starting point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower – Cerro de la Cabrica Viewpoint loop from Torre de Cope&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-kilometer loop that offers historical interest and great views.

Q: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can we visit along the trails?
A: The region is rich in points of interest. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;, a 16th-century watchtower offering panoramic views. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6331635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calabardina&lt;/a&gt;, both providing stunning coastal vistas. Many trails also lead to beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4858540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galera Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cabo Cope Y Puntas De Calnegre?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience here, with an average score of 4.36 stars from over 500 ratings. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal scenery, the variety of landscapes, and the opportunity to combine hiking with relaxing on beautiful, often secluded, beaches.

Q: Are there any family-friendly routes that offer views of Cabo Cope?
A: Yes, for a route with excellent views of Cabo Cope, consider the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31553522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cabo Cope – View of Isla del Fraile loop from Calabardina&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.6-kilometer trail provides magnificent perspectives of the headland and the surrounding sea.

Q: Are there any moderate family hikes for slightly older children or more active families?
A: For families seeking a bit more challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galera Beach loop from Playa del Hoyo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option. This 11.8-kilometer route offers a longer exploration of the coastal area, perfect for an active day out.

Q: What is the terrain like on these family-friendly trails?
A: The terrain is varied, reflecting the region's diverse geology. You can expect a mix of sandy paths along beaches, rocky sections on coastal trails, and some dirt tracks through scrubland. The easy routes generally have well-defined paths, while moderate trails might include more uneven surfaces and slight inclines.

Q: Are there any trails that start near the historic Cope Tower?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17243451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cabrica Viewpoint – Cope Tower loop from Torre de Cope&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4-kilometer route that begins and ends near the historic Cope Tower, allowing you to explore the area's history and natural beauty in one go.
tips: Mirador Cerro de la Cabrica is located in Águilas, Murcia, and is an ideal place to enjoy stunning panoramic views. It is a popular destination for hikers and nature lovers. There are several hiking routes that take you to the viewpoint, offering different levels of difficulty.
Nice, pleasant and short round trip linear hiking route around the Murcian town of Calabardina.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Return to Cocón de Cope (PR-MU 104) trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While generally accessible, some sections are rocky and require good physical fitness and sure-footedness. It's suitable for most skill levels, but be prepared for varying ground conditions.

faqs.question: Is the PR-MU 104 a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Return to Cocón de Cope (PR-MU 104) is a circular route, meaning you'll end up back near your starting point. It also offers several derivations if you wish to adapt your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a varied landscape, moving along paths and trails with diverse surfaces. While generally accessible, some sections may be rocky and require careful footing. You'll experience a mix of Mediterranean mountains, beaches, cliffs, salt marshes, and dunes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on the trail?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the panoramic views from Cocón de Cope, which is the highest point of the route. You'll also pass by the 16th-century Torre de Cope, ancient archaeological sites, and the Cerro de la Cabrica Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna can I spot along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in biodiversity. You might see flora such as Bermuda oxalis, Almería helianthemum, maritime asterolide, and prickly pear. For fauna, look out for reptiles like the Iberian skink and ocellated lizard, and birds including Bonelli's eagle and peregrine falcon.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the PR-MU 104 trail?
faqs.answer: The best times are typically spring and autumn. It's strongly recommended to avoid hiking during the central hours of the day in summer due to extreme heat. Always carry plenty of water regardless of the season.

faqs.question: Is the Regional Park of Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre — Return to Cocón de Cope (PR-MU 104) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the park is a natural area, specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Regional Park of Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre?
faqs.answer: The Regional Park of Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre is a protected natural area (Area of Community Interest and Special Protection Area for Birds). Generally, there are no entrance fees or permits required for hiking on established trails like the PR-MU 104. However, always respect the natural environment and follow any posted regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Regional Park of Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre. While a specific parking lot for the PR-MU 104 is not detailed, trailheads within natural parks typically have designated parking areas. Look for signage upon arrival at the park entrance or near the start of the route.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the PR-MU 104 trail intersects with other well-known paths. Approximately 30% of the route covers sections of the Sendero del Mediterráneo - Murcia, and about 29% overlaps with the Transmurciana 08 trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's essential to wear appropriate footwear suitable for rocky sections. Always carry plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Sun protection, snacks, and a map or GPS device are also recommended.
